summary: Keep approval reviewed-period data visible during refresh and replace full-page loading with skeleton states.

notes: No database schema, RLS, RPC, or function grant changes are included in this release.
This is a frontend-only approval-center loading experience release.
Approval reviewed period queries now keep previous data while the selected period refreshes.
Desktop approval tasks show a structured skeleton only on first load instead of replacing the whole area with plain loading text.
Mobile approval tasks use matching skeleton cards on first load.
Reviewed period changes now keep the existing list visible and show a low-noise update badge while fresh data is loading.
If a refresh fails while previous data exists, the approval center keeps the previous data visible and shows a scoped warning.

verification: Run npm --prefix frontend run build with VITE_APP_VERSION=V0.18.30 and production VITE variables.
Run deploy/scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh on the cloud host with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.30.
Run scripts/smoke-frontend-render.mjs against production with EXPECTED_VERSION=V0.18.30.
known_warnings: Vite may still warn that some chunks are larger than 500 kB.
No approval workflow semantics were changed; only loading and refresh presentation changed.
